title: gypsophila perfoliata var. tomentosa
intro: Gypsophila perfoliata var. tomentosa, commonly known as woolly gypsophila, is a plant species in the family Caryophyllaceae, characterized by its woolly leaves and stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of woolly gypsophila include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies for skin issues and infections. In herbal preparations, woolly gypsophila is often used in the form of infusions, tinctures, or salves, and can be applied topically to affected areas to harness its medicinal properties.
